title: Common Types
description: Shared primitives. Deviation B1 from product-spec.md §24: an 18th schema beyond the 17 listed, because percentile sets, money, and simulation references are used by most other schemas. See docs/open-questions.md B.

money.description: USD. Never defaulted to zero when unknown; use null.

confidence.description: Contributing factors are reported individually so the user can see which input is weak, not just the total.

percentileSet.description: Implements D1 and D2. Percentiles are taken on this quantity's OWN distribution (convention OWN_DISTRIBUTION_V1). Risk direction per quantity is normative in docs/forecast-definitions.md §1.1. simulation_id identifies the draw matrix this was summarized from; two percentile sets may be combined arithmetically only when their simulation_id values match (§12.5).

censored_above.description: Simulation horizon. Percentiles at or above this are censored, not extrapolated (docs/forecast-definitions.md §3.4).

simulationRef.description: Reproducibility stamp. Given identical inputs and versions, output is byte-identical.

model_label.description: product-spec.md §16.2. Must not be represented as a trained production prediction.

sourceMeta.description: Provenance envelope. Required by §21 freshness checks and §23 audit.

fieldProvenance.description: Per-field origin, per §13.3. A value the agent inferred rather than read is ESTIMATED and raises INSUFFICIENT_VEHICLE_DATA when it feeds a floor calculation.

explanationInputs.description: The narration allow-list (docs/architecture.md §3.4). The explanation layer receives ONLY these values. A currency figure or duration in generated narrative that does not appear here fails the response rather than reaching the user. This is what enforces §4.1 at the output boundary.

risk_direction.description: Prevents a narrative quoting the optimistic tail of a loss-bearing quantity.
